title: ruppia cirrhosa
intro: Ruppia cirrhosa, also known as tape-grass or sea-needle-grass, is a type of submerged aquatic plant that belongs to the family Potamogetonaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing coughs, relieving colds, and soothing sore throats. In herbal preparations, Ruppia cirrhosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
